name: Rejuvant retrospective cohort reported lower DNA-methylation age
summary: The Rejuvant report is a large claimed epigenetic-age shift, but it is retrospective, uncontrolled, and tied to a commercial formulation and test.
statement: A retrospective 42-person analysis reported lower DNA-methylation age after four to ten months of an alpha-ketoglutarate and vitamin formulation.
time_horizon: Four to ten months, averaging about seven months
quantitative_note: The abstract reports an average 8-year decrease in biological age by the TruAge DNA methylation test.
caveats: The study was retrospective and lacked placebo or randomized controls.
The endpoint used a commercial DNA-methylation test, and the product sponsor context creates interpretation risk.
Health, function, disease, and mortality endpoints were not established.
The result should be treated as hypothesis-generating until placebo-controlled replication exists.
